name: Uthman ibn Ali
title: Son of Umm al-Banin
shortDescription: The 21-year-old brother of Hazrat Abbas (AS) who, at the command of his older brother, stepped into the battlefield and sacrificed his life for the Imam.
fullStory:
Uthman ibn Ali ibn Abi Talib is one of the four brave sons of Lady Umm al-Banin (SA). Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) named him after the noble companion of the Prophet, Uthman ibn Maz'un. He was a handsome and valiant 21-year-old youth who inherited bravery from his father, Haidar al-Karrar.

On the day of Ashura, when Imam Hussain's (AS) companions were martyred one after another, it was Bani Hashem's turn. Hazrat Abbas (AS), who commanded Bani Hashem, turned to his brothers (Abdullah, Uthman, and Ja'far) and said: 'O my brothers, you have no children; go to the battlefield before me and fight so that I may witness your sacrifice and martyrdom in the way of God and for our brother'.

Uthman, following his brother Abdullah, bravely entered the battlefield. He recited epic war poetry, introducing himself as the son of Ali (AS). Despite his young age, Uthman fought with Haidari skill and killed a group of Kufan soldiers.

Khawali ibn Yazid al-Asbahi, who was cowardly watching his bravery from afar, shot an arrow at him. The arrow hit Uthman's side, throwing him from his horse. Meanwhile, a man from the Bani Darim tribe came forward and severed the head of this brave Alawi youth.

The martyrdom of Uthman and his brothers before Hazrat Abbas (AS) demonstrates the unparalleled insight and devotion of Umm al-Banin's (SA) children, who made themselves shields for the Master of Martyrs (AS).
quoteArabic: تَقَدَّموا حَتّى أَراكُمْ قَدْ نَصَحْتُمْ لِلَّهِ وَلِرَسولِهِ
quote: (Abbas to him:) Go forth so that I may see you have sacrificed for God and His Messenger.
